filter_applied: Strict Malta-relevance filter: all items have direct connection to Malta as victim, attacker, regulator or legal jurisdiction

items.title: MGA Breach: Wittmann Claims Responsibility via Twitter/X, Threatens Full iGaming Archive Release
items.summary: Lilith Wittmann of the Chaos Computer Club claimed responsibility for breaching the MGA, posting directly to X: "Yes, I hacked you, and the data obtained has been shared with media partners, authorities." She alleged the MGA enabled organised crime schemes and warned any extradition attempt would trigger immediate release of her full iGaming data archive covering all 304 licensed operators. The MGA confirmed its systems were accessed by someone who "presented" as a security researcher and opened a criminal investigation.
items.malta_connection: MGA is Malta's primary gaming regulator; breach of its internal systems; attacker publicly targeted MGA via social media

items.title: NEW — Evos Oil Terminal Birzebbuga Hit by Ransomware in European-Wide Attack
items.summary: The Evos oil storage terminal in Birzebbuga, Malta — acquired from Oiltanking the previous year — suffered IT disruptions as part of a coordinated ransomware wave hitting European oil and fuel terminals in January–February 2022. Evos confirmed "disruption of IT services" at its Malta facility causing "delays in execution", though all operations continued safely. The attack, attributed to the BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware group, simultaneously hit Oiltanking and Mabanaft in Germany and SEA-Invest in Belgium, affecting 17 European port terminals.
items.malta_connection: Evos operates Malta's Birzebbuga oil storage terminal — part of Malta's critical energy infrastructure — directly impacted by the ransomware attack

items.title: IDPC Enforcement Surge: CCTV Overreach and Subject Access Request Failures Key Themes in 2024–2025
items.summary: Malta's IDPC significantly stepped up enforcement activity in 2024 and 2025, with two recurring themes: failures to properly handle subject access requests under Articles 5 and 15 GDPR, and CCTV surveillance overreach where cameras captured public spaces or third-party property without justification. Financial penalties were imposed in more intrusive cases, signalling the IDPC's willingness to escalate beyond corrective orders. The IDPC is now also tasked with maintaining Malta's registry of high-risk AI systems under the EU AI Act.
items.malta_connection: IDPC is Malta's data protection authority; decisions directly affect Malta-based data controllers and processors

items.title: BOV Launches 'Spot the Scam' Self-Assessment Tool as Fraud Losses Near €5.4M
items.summary: Bank of Valletta launched a free online 'Spot the Scam Self-Assessment' tool for Maltese consumers in April 2026, amid fraud losses that nearly doubled from €2.9M in 2024 to €5.4M in just the first eight months of 2025. The tool helps users identify warning signs of phishing, social engineering and investment scams. Separately, Newsbook confirmed that 28.7% of Maltese enterprises reported a cyber incident in 2023 — well above the EU average of 21.5% (Eurostat 2024).
items.malta_connection: BOV is Malta's largest bank; tool targets Maltese consumers; fraud statistics specific to Malta

items.title: Multi-Bank Phishing Scheme: 100+ Victims Across BOV, APS, HSBC and BNF Bank — Suspect Arrested
items.summary: A coordinated phishing operation using fake bank apps and SMS links mimicking BOV's login portal defrauded 100+ victims across four Maltese banks between September 2024 and May 2025. The scheme spread from BOV to APS Bank, then HSBC Malta and BNF Bank. A 25-year-old suspect was arrested in February 2026 and denied bail, with investigators detecting international involvement and cryptocurrency money laundering via Revolut and Bitcoin transactions.
items.malta_connection: Four Malta-licensed banks targeted; victims are Maltese bank customers; suspect arrested by Malta Police

items.title: MFSA Issues 'Heightened Cyber Threat' Circular to All Supervised Financial Entities
items.summary: The Malta Financial Services Authority issued a circular titled 'Exercising Caution in Times of Heightened Cyber Threat' on 5 March 2026, warning all MFSA-supervised entities of an elevated cyber risk environment. The circular followed the MFSA's 2026 Supervisory Priorities which place ICT and cyber-resilience frameworks under DORA as a top concern for Malta's entire financial sector. Entities were urged to conduct situational awareness assessments and revisit ICT risk management procedures.
items.malta_connection: MFSA is Malta's financial regulator; circular addressed to all Malta-licensed financial entities

items.title: Fast Track CRM Cyberattack Exposes Casino Player KYC Documents Including Passports
items.summary: Malta-headquartered iGaming CRM provider Fast Track was breached in October 2025, exposing player KYC documents including passports and driver's licences across at least two casino operators. Shuffle Casino was the only publicly named victim and terminated its partnership with Fast Track. The breach occurred despite Fast Track holding a freshly renewed SOC 2 Type 2 certification from June 2025, highlighting certification limitations.
items.malta_connection: Fast Track is headquartered in Malta; MGA-licensed operators affected; breach reported to Maltese authorities

items.title: Saint James Hospital — Malta's First Confirmed Healthcare Cyberattack
items.summary: Saint James Hospital Group confirmed a sophisticated cyberattack on 18 April 2025, immediately isolating all systems from external networks. A forensic investigation was launched into potential patient data exposure by IT, legal and external cybersecurity experts. Both the IDPC and Malta Police were formally notified — the first confirmed cyberattack on Maltese healthcare infrastructure.
items.malta_connection: Saint James Hospital is Malta-based; patients are Maltese residents; IDPC and Malta Police formally notified

items.title: MTCA Email Error Exposes Tax IDs and Contact Details of ~7,000 Companies
items.summary: The Malta Tax and Customs Administration accidentally attached a file with company names, tax identification numbers, registration numbers and contact details of approximately 7,000 Malta-registered businesses to a mass email sent to unintended recipients. The IDPC was formally notified. The Opposition demanded Finance Minister accountability and an independent investigation.
items.malta_connection: MTCA is a Maltese government authority; 7,000 Malta-registered companies' data exposed; IDPC Malta notified

items.title: MSS Annual Reports: Phishing, Ransomware and DDoS Surged During Malta's UN and OSCE Tenure
items.source: MaltaToday (MSS Annual Reports 2023–2024 tabled in Parliament)
items.summary: Malta's Security Service annual reports for 2023 and 2024, tabled in Parliament in 2025, confirmed a surge in cyberattacks during Malta's UN Security Council and OSCE chairmanship periods. Incidents included phishing targeting public sector employees, ransomware on SMEs, and DDoS attacks. The MSS found no direct causal link to diplomatic activity but noted Malta's elevated geopolitical profile likely increased its cyber-risk exposure.
items.malta_connection: MSS is Malta's national security service; attacks targeted Maltese public sector employees and Malta-based SMEs
